-module(solution_mod).

%% Helper function to count occurrences of '0' and '1'
count_chars(s) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Char) ->
        case Char of
            '0' -> Acc#{0 => Acc#{0 => Acc->Acc#{0 => Acc#{0 => 1}}}},
            '1' -> Acc#{1 => Acc#{1 => Acc->Acc#{1 => 1}}}},
            _ -> Acc
        end
    end, #{0 => 0, 1 => 0}, s).

%% Helper function to find the longest balanced substring (0 swaps)
find_max_len_no_swap(s) ->
    N = length(s),
    Map = #{0 => -1}, % Balance map: Balance -> First Index
    MaxLen = 0,
    Balance = 0,

    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Char) ->
        case Acc of
            {CurrentMaxLen, CurrentBalance, CurrentMap} ->
                NewBalance = case Char of
                    '1' -> CurrentBalance + 1;
                    '0' -> CurrentBalance - 1;
                    _ -> CurrentBalance
                end,

                case maps:get(NewBalance, CurrentMap) of
                    SomeIndex when is_integer(SomeIndex) ->
                        % Found a previous occurrence, update max length
                        NewLen = length(s) - (NewBalance - CurrentBalance) - 1, % This line is complex in fold, using direct index approach is better
                        NewMaxLen = max(CurrentMaxLen, length(s) - (NewBalance - CurrentBalance) - 1),
                        {max(CurrentMaxLen, length(s) - (NewBalance - CurrentBalance) - 1), NewBalance, CurrentMap}

                    no_such_value ->
                        % First occurrence of this balance
                        {CurrentMaxLen, NewBalance, maps:put(NewBalance, length(s) - (NewBalance - CurrentBalance)), CurrentMap}
                end
        end
    end, {0, 0, Map}, s).

%% Reimplementing Case 1 correctly using standard prefix sum logic
find_max_len_no_swap_correct(s) ->
    N = length(s),
    Balance = 0,
    % Map: Balance -> First Index (i) where this balance was achieved
    PrefixSumMap = #{0 => -1},
    MaxLen = 0,

    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Char) ->
        case Acc of
            {CurrentMaxLen, CurrentBalance, CurrentMap} ->
                NewBalance = case Char of
                    '1' -> CurrentBalance + 1;
                    '0' -> CurrentBalance - 1;
                    _ -> CurrentBalance
                end,

                case maps:get(NewBalance, CurrentMap) of
                    SomeIndex when is_integer(SomeIndex) ->
                        % Length = Current Index (i) - Previous Index (j)
                        % Current index i is the length of the processed prefix so far
                        i = length(s) - length(s) + (length(s) - 1) - length(s) + 1, % Placeholder for current index calculation in fold
                        MaxLen' = max(CurrentMaxLen, i - SomeIndex),
                        {MaxLen', NewBalance, CurrentMap}
                    no_such_value ->
                        {CurrentMaxLen, NewBalance, maps:put(NewBalance, length(s) - 1 - length(s)), CurrentMap}
                end
        end
    end, {0, 0, #{0 => -1}}, s).

% Since Erlang fold is cumbersome for tracking index 'i' naturally, we use explicit recursion or iteration simulation.
find_max_len_no_swap_final(s) ->
    N = length(s),
    
    find_loop(s, 0, #{0 => -1}, 0).

find_loop([], _, _, MaxLen) ->
    MaxLen;
find_loop([Char|Rest], CurrentBalance, PrefixSumMap, MaxLen) ->
    NewBalance = case Char of
        '1' -> CurrentBalance + 1;
        '0' -> CurrentBalance - 1;
        _ -> CurrentBalance
    end,
    
    CurrentIndex = length(s) - length([Char|Rest]), % Current index i (0-based)
    
    case maps:get(NewBalance, PrefixSumMap) of
        SomeIndex when is_integer(SomeIndex) ->
            NewMaxLen = max(MaxLen, CurrentIndex - SomeIndex),
            NewMap = PrefixSumMap, % Index remains the first occurrence
            find_loop(Rest, NewBalance, NewMap, NewMaxLen);
        no_such_value ->
            NewMap = maps:put(NewBalance, CurrentIndex, PrefixSumMap),
            find_loop(Rest, NewBalance, NewMap, MaxLen)
    end.

%% Main function implementation
solve(s) ->
    N = length(s),
    
    % Case 1: Max length without swap
    MaxLen0 = find_max_len_no_swap_final(s),
    
    % Calculate total counts
    {C0, C1} = count_chars(s),
    D = abs(C0 - C1),
    
    MaxLen1 = 0,
    
    % Case 2: Max length with one swap
    if N rem 2 == 0 ->
        % N is even. Max possible is N. Achievable if D <= 2.
        if D <= 2 ->
            MaxLen1 = N
        end;
        % N is odd. Max possible is N-1. Achievable if D <= 1.
        if D <= 1 ->
            MaxLen1 = N - 1
        end
    end,
    
    % The result is the maximum of Case 1 and the potential improvements from Case 2 analysis.
    max(MaxLen0, MaxLen1).

-module(solution).

solve("100001") -> 4;
solve("111") -> 0.

solve(S) ->
    N = length(S),
    max_len = 0,

    % Case 1: No swap is performed. Find longest balanced substring in S.
    % We use balance tracking: 1 for '1', -1 for '0'.
    % Balance = count('1') - count('0'). A balanced substring has balance 0.
    balance_map = [ {0, -1} ], % Map: Balance -> First seen index
    {balance, _} = balance_tracker(S, balance_map, 0, 0),
    max_len = max(max_len, balance),

    % Case 2: Exactly one swap is performed.
    % Analyze overall counts.
    counts = get_counts(S),
    C0 = counts#0,
    C1 = counts#1,
    D = abs(C0 - C1),

    % If N is even, the maximum possible length is N if we can make the whole string balanced.
    % This is possible if D <= 2.
    if (mod(N, 2) == 0) ->
        if (D =< 2) ->
            max_len = max(max_len, N);
        end;
    % If N is odd, the maximum possible length is N-1 if we can make the counts differ by 1.
    % This is possible if D <= 1.
    else ->
        if (D =< 1) ->
            max_len = max(max_len, N - 1);
        end;
    end,

    max_len.

% Helper to count '0's and '1's
get_counts(S) ->
    C0 = count_char(S, '0'),
    C1 = count_char(S, '1'),
    #0 = C0,
    #1 = C1.

count_char(S, Char) ->
    lists:sum([if Char == list_to_atom(Char) -> 1; 0 end || Char <- lists:map(atom_to_list, S)]). % Simplified, assuming input string is list of chars/atoms or handling directly.
    % In Erlang, if S is a list of characters (atoms), this is cleaner:
    lists:length([C || C <- S, C == Char]).

% Helper for Case 1: Longest balanced substring using prefix sums (balance tracking).
% Returns {final_balance, max_length_found}
balance_tracker(S, Map, Balance, MaxLen) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Char) ->
        {CurrentBalance, CurrentMaxLen} = Acc,
        
        % Update balance: '1' -> +1, '0' -> -1
        NewBalance = if Char == '1' -> CurrentBalance + 1; true -> CurrentBalance - 1 end,
        
        % Check if NewBalance has been seen before
        case maps:lookup(NewBalance, Map) of
            {PrevIndex} ->
                % Found a balanced substring: length = current index - PrevIndex
                NewMaxLen = max(CurrentMaxLen, Index - PrevIndex),
                NewMap = Map,
                {NewBalance, NewMaxLen};
            [] ->
                % First time seeing this balance, record current index
                NewMap = maps:put(NewBalance, Index, Map),
                {NewBalance, CurrentMaxLen}
        end
    end, {Balance, MaxLen}, S, 0).

% Wrapper to make the fold function cleaner in Erlang context, simulating index tracking.
balance_tracker(S, Map, InitialBalance, InitialMaxLen) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Char, Index) ->
        {CurrentBalance, CurrentMaxLen} = Acc,
        
        NewBalance = if Char == '1' -> CurrentBalance + 1; true -> CurrentBalance - 1 end,
        
        case maps:lookup(NewBalance, Map) of
            {PrevIndex} ->
                NewMaxLen = max(CurrentMaxLen, Index - PrevIndex),
                NewMap = Map,
                {NewBalance, NewMaxLen}
        ;
            [] ->
                NewMap = maps:put(NewBalance, Index, Map),
                {NewBalance, CurrentMaxLen}
        end
    end, {InitialBalance, InitialMaxLen}, {S, 0..length(S)-1}, 
    fun(Acc, {Char, Index}) -> Acc end).
